campaign: A3 — Autonomous /vision+/campaign drive for systemic escalations

phase_outcomes.P1: Drive-session skeleton. DriveRunner (mirror ImplementRunner) spawns a headless session that produces a vision .md + declares epicName+campaigns in PM_DRIVE_STATUS_PATH sentinel; finish() STRUCTURAL SEAL = file-on-disk cross-check (path-escape guard + fs.stat isFile) — vision_ready never inferred. LOOP does ALL PM I/O over HTTP (createEpic+createTask per campaign + addMessage(pendingDrive) acknowledged) because the worktree clone has no project-management MCP (.mcp.json gitignored — the defect that ESCALATED the first plan; resolved per the vision's commander-authority inline-orchestration option). Replaces the A1 systemic needs_human placeholder. Partial-failure (epic ok, task fails) → escalateToHuman naming the orphan epic, never stranded acknowledged. Reclaim skips pendingDrive. 151 responder tests (+30). build/typecheck/lint green. Ships enabled=false. dc3e978.
phase_outcomes.P3: Checkpoint/resume (daemon-restart survival). The ONE real code change: an EARLY pre-epic intent marker in runDriveSession's vision_ready case — addMessage(escalationId, '<drive starting>', 'diagnosis', {pendingDrive:true, visionPath}) with NO epicId, written BEFORE createEpic; the existing terminal marker UPGRADES it with {epicId} after the tasks. This closes the duplicate-arc window: if the daemon dies AFTER createEpic but BEFORE the terminal marker, on restart the reclaim probe finds the intent marker (hasPendingDriveMarker) → routes to advanceArc → arcEpicId returns null (intent marker has no epicId) → the EXISTING null-epic branch (~1229) escalateToHuman recovers the rare orphan epic; NO second createEpic fires. arcEpicId-null→escalateToHuman was CONFIRMED already wired (no adjustment needed). Gaps 2-5 were already structurally closed (advanceArc derives arc state STRICTLY from the server each cycle — getEpic tasks + listMergeRequests(escalationId) — so a fresh createResponderState() is a faithful restart simulator; ResponderState is entirely transient); added SEALING tests, not code. The server-side markers + merge_requests ARE the checkpoint — NO progress.json checkpoint file, NO schema column, NO findArcEpicForEscalation api-client method. 7 new P3 tests (restart-resumes-next-phase, no-duplicate-epic-on-intent-marker, intent-marker-written-before-createEpic ordering via mock.invocationCallOrder, completed-arc-not-re-driven, no-double-submit-across-ticks, checkpoint-round-trips-fresh-state, byte-identical-disabled); 2 existing P1 tests updated (addMessage count now reflects the early intent marker: vision_ready writes 2 markers; createTask-fail-mid-loop writes the 1 intent marker before the orphan escalate). 168 responder tests (was 161 — +7 net). build/typecheck/lint green. Ships enabled=false.
phase_outcomes.P4: Close-on-completion. advanceArc's arc_complete not-yet-marked sub-branch now RESOLVES the escalation as holder: addMessage(arcComplete marker) FIRST (idempotence gate — the hasArcCompleteMarker early-return keys off it so a marked thread never resolves twice), then answer→resolve (mirrors A2's postBackToEscalation: `if detail.status===acknowledged answer(summary); resolve(summary)`). Summary names the epic NAME + landed shas. Origin auto-notices via C2 (listUndeliveredForWorker surfaces the holder answer diagnosis + resolve system messages, ne(authorId, originAuthor), seq>cursor). resolve fires once: resolved is terminal + listAcknowledgedByHolder filters status=acknowledged so a resolved arc is never re-seeded. api-client gains resolve(id, reason)→POST /escalations/{id}/resolve (server route+resolveBody+service already existed — NO server/schema/openapi change). No-recursion structural (phase MRs self-authored + taskId-gated + resolved terminal). The answer→resolve strand (death between the 2 HTTP calls → strand at answered, un-seeded) ACCEPTED for P4: benign (all phases landed + origin already C2-noticed the answer summary; only formal resolved status lost); matches the shipped A2 precedent; atomic seal deferred to P5/A4. 170 responder tests (+2). build/typecheck/lint green. 54df330.
phase_outcomes.P5: Seal + campaign close. NO new capability, NO source/REST change (git diff e5323b5..HEAD over src/openapi/web/src/api empty; no packages/*/src diff) — docs + a full-stack seal test only. (a) Extended packages/server/tests/responder-seal.test.ts with a NEW `A3 autonomous-drive arc seal` describe block reusing the A2 block's helpers wholesale (silentLogger, ResponderClient+fetchImpl=app.request, createTestApp/Project/AiAgent/authRequest, FakeWorktree, raiseOpen, the real-HTTP land/reject idioms, the C2 undelivered+mark-delivered idiom). New local fakes: SystemicDeclaringRunner (implement{systemic}), VisionDriveRunner (real DriveRunResult vision_ready{visionPath,epicName,campaigns×2}, NO epicId — the loop mints the epic over HTTP), BranchReadyRunner (per-phase branch_ready). CRITICAL: the deps set driveRunner explicitly (required ResponderDeps field the A2 helper omits) + an injected mutable clock advanced +5_000_000ms per tick (≫ the reclaim staleness gate timeBudgetSec900+grace120=1_020_000ms) so the reclaim pass re-finds the self-held acknowledged escalation and routes it to advanceArc each tick. Seal 1 (the headline) drives the REAL responderTick end-to-end: tick1 assess→systemic→drive vision_ready→loop creates the REAL epic+2 tasks over HTTP+intent+terminal pendingDrive markers (asserted: acknowledged/held; 1 epic+2 child tasks; terminal marker carries the real epicId); tick2 advanceArc implements+submits the phase-1 task-LINKED+escalationId MR (branch pm/escalation-<id>-<taskId>); LAND it over real HTTP → THE LOAD-BEARING GATE: the escalation is STILL acknowledged (the taskId!==null post-back skip — provable ONLY against the real land service); tick3 phase-2 implement+submit+land; tick4 all-landed→arcComplete marker→answer→resolve as holder with a summary naming the epic NAME + both shas (asserted resolved + resolvedBy===responder); C2 origin-notice surfaces then mark-delivered drains. The phase task is derived from the submitted MR (newestPhaseMr), NOT presupposed order (the tasks list is created_at-desc; ties are non-deterministic). Seal 2 (arc_partial): through phase-1 land, then phase-2 REJECT (test_failed) → tick4 advanceArc → arc_partial → escalateToHuman the ROOT (needs_human) with the landed sha + the rejected phase title; phase-1's landed MR+branch untouched (no rollback), the rejected task never re-submitted (exactly 1 MR/task). Both `it`s carry an explicit 30000ms timeout (4 ticks × real HTTP + on-demand TS transpile exceed the 5s default — the only friction; logic passes in ~5-6s/test). (b) CLAUDE.md A3 paragraph inserted after the A1+A2 paragraph. (c) packages/responder-ref/README.md gained an `Autonomous drive (Campaign A3)` section (numbered flow mirroring A1) + fixed the stale line (~87) `systemic always escalates to a human` → `systemic drives the autonomous arc`. VERIFY: server 1712 (+2 A3 seals, full suite green), responder 170 (unchanged — no source), shared 513; build/typecheck/lint all green; openapi/web diff empty (confirmed). Ships auto_implement.enabled=false. Campaign A3 COMPLETE.
phase_outcomes.P2: Tick-driven campaign-phase drive (advanceArc), Model A. A self-held acknowledged escalation carrying pendingDrive/pendingArc is re-found by the reclaim pass each cycle and ROUTED to advanceArc (replacing P1's reclaim-SKIP of pendingDrive) at the marker-skip site. advanceArc derives arc state STRICTLY FROM THE SERVER — getEpic(campaign-tasks) + listMergeRequests(escalationId) matched by taskId — and does ONE of: re-park (a phase MR queued/integrating; one phase in flight max) | arc_partial (a phase MR rejected/abandoned/orphaned → escalateToHuman the ROOT naming landed shas + remaining; TERMINAL, no re-submit) | arc_complete (all landed → arcComplete marker, NOT resolve — P4's job; idempotent via hasArcCompleteMarker) | implement next phase (reuse runImplementForBranch: branch pm/escalation-<id>-<taskId>, phase brief, task-LINKED escalationId MR, pendingArc handoff). THE CRUX = Directive 1: gated BOTH land + reject post-backs in merge-request.service.ts on row.taskId===null so a phase MR threads escalationId for attribution WITHOUT an early phase resolving the root (only advanceArc drives completion/partial). Directive 2: advanceArc reserves spawn budget (canSpawn/recordSpawn) but NEVER reclaimAttempts (a >2-phase arc would poison-cap mid-drive). Refactored runImplementSession→shared runImplementForBranch(ImplementPhaseConfig); buildImplementPrompt gains optional PhaseBrief; api-client gains listMergeRequests + getEpic (epic + tasks via ?epic= list); merge-requests list route+service gain escalationId filter (openapi+web regen). 161 responder tests (+10), 1710 server tests (+3 Directive-1). A2 post-back tests stayed green (task-LESS bounded MR unaffected). build/typecheck/lint green. Ships enabled=false.

notes: CAMPAIGN A3 COMPLETE (P1–P5 all committed). P5 sealed the arc: a full-stack responder-seal driving the REAL responderTick through assess→systemic→drive→per-phase implement+land→resolve→C2, with the load-bearing taskId===null post-back gate proven against the real land service (a phase land leaves the escalation acknowledged; only advanceArc resolves the root), plus the arc_partial reject seal (needs_human, proven phases preserved, no rollback). Docs: CLAUDE.md A3 paragraph + responder README A3 section (+ stale-line fix). NO source/REST change — additive, ships auto_implement.enabled=false. Below: P4 history retained. P4: escalation↔arc linkage + close-on-completion. (1) On ARC COMPLETION (P2 emits the arcComplete marker leaving the escalation acknowledged), P4 RESOLVES the escalation AS THE HOLDER (the A2 resolve-as-holder pattern: answer→resolve through legal transitions) with the shipped summary (landed shas + vision epic); origin auto-notices via C2. On give_up/budget-exhaustion → needs_human with the partial-progress payload (no proven work discarded — already partly done by advanceArc's arc_partial). (2) The escalation tracks the arc end-to-end — each phase land posts progress to the thread (reuse the marker messages); decide whether an epic↔escalation durable field is needed for the resolve summary or the existing markers suffice (trust-first: least machinery). (3) No-recursion across the multi-phase arc: a drive-authored MR / any escalation its landing spawns never re-triggers auto-implement (the A2 structural seal extends — phase MRs are escalation-linked + author/origin checks hold). P4 CONSUMES P2's arcComplete marker. The resolve must run from the LOOP (advanceArc's arc_complete branch currently only emits the marker — P4 adds the resolve there or in a dedicated close path). Trust-first; ships enabled=false; main never breaks. Then P5 = full-stack seal + CLAUDE.md/README + campaign close.
